content_of_post_to_answer: How does stress affect memory and concentration?
answer: Stress doesn't just make memory worse; it changes what the brain prioritizes. Under stress, your brain shifts resources toward detecting threats and solving immediate problems, which leaves less capacity for forming and retrieving memories. That's why people often say, "I read the page three times and retained nothing." The issue isn't always memory itself—it's that attention never fully arrived in the first place. I've explored this attention-memory connection in more detail on my Quora profile if anyone finds it interesting. A lot of what we call forgetfulness is actually overloaded attention wearing a memory mask.
